name: Python Web Frameworks GitHub Stars Analysis 2026
description: Search for the 5 most popular Python web frameworks in 2026, retrieve their GitHub star counts, create a text-based bar chart ranking, and save the complete report to markdown

tasks.name: Search for top 5 Python web frameworks
tasks.description: You MUST call web_search with the query 'top 5 most popular Python web frameworks 2026'. Output the FULL raw search results verbatim (URLs, titles, snippets). Do NOT summarize as 'results were found' — downstream tasks need the actual framework names. Extract and list the 5 framework names explicitly. Output format: First list the 5 framework names, then include the complete search results with URLs and snippets.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains exactly 5 Python framework names (e.g., Django, Flask, FastAPI) and includes raw search results with at least 3 URLs

tasks.name: Find GitHub star counts for all frameworks
tasks.description: You MUST call web_search multiple times (once per framework) to find GitHub star counts. For each of the 5 frameworks from task_001, search '<framework_name> github stars 2026' or '<framework_name> github repository'. Output the FULL raw search results for each framework. Do NOT invent star counts — extract them from search result snippets. If no star count found, write 'not found'. Output format: For each framework, write 'Framework: <name>, Stars: <number>, Source: <URL>' followed by the raw search snippets.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains numeric star counts for at least 4 of the 5 frameworks, each with a cited source URL from the search results

tasks.name: Generate text-based bar chart with Python
tasks.description: You MUST call python_exec to create a text-based bar chart. Write Python code that: (1) parses task_002 output to extract framework names and star counts as integers, (2) sorts frameworks by stars descending, (3) generates a text bar chart using '█' characters scaled to the highest count, (4) formats output as 'FrameworkName: ██████ (123,456 stars)' with one framework per line. The code must print the complete chart. Do NOT use matplotlib or images — text output only.
tasks.success_criteria: Output is a text-based bar chart with 5 lines, each showing framework name, visual bars using █ characters, and star count in parentheses, sorted from highest to lowest stars

tasks.name: Save complete report to markdown file
tasks.description: You MUST call file_write to save to 'python_frameworks_report.md'. Combine all previous task outputs into a markdown document with: (1) title '# Python Web Frameworks Popularity Report 2026', (2) section '## Rankings' with the bar chart from task_003, (3) section '## Detailed Data' with framework names, GitHub URLs, and exact star counts from task_002. The file_path parameter must be exactly 'python_frameworks_report.md'.
tasks.success_criteria: File python_frameworks_report.md exists and contains markdown with a title, the bar chart visualization, and data table showing all 5 frameworks with their star counts
